derived_from.hypothesis: A social outing causing poor sleep and a rest day is a training-relevant life disruption that should be logged to log.md without an explicit request.

intent: When the user shares that they went out, slept poorly, and are resting today, chat should propose appending it to log.md and still reply concisely.

context.me:
# Profile

- Adam, 42M, 81 kg.
- Wife + 3-year-old; live in Crosshaven, Ireland.
context.strategy:
# Strategy

## Goals — Current focus

- Hold 3 runs + 2 lifts/week at 15+ km volume.
- Build tempo capacity: extend tempo segments toward 3 km @ sub-5:10/km.

## Weekly Plan

**Week runs Monday → Sunday.** Target: 3 runs + 2 strength sessions per week (~15 km+ total running).

- Run days: ~5 km each
  - 1 tempo session/week when HRV is above personal baseline (~50 ms)
  - Remaining runs at easy pace (5:30–6:00/km)
- Strength A: Push + core
- Strength B: Pull + core
- Prefer not to stack run + strength on the same day
context.log:
# Log

- 2026-04-09 [rest son sick] — skipped planned strength
- 2026-04-10 [strength b done] — slept better, felt strong

turns.content: Went out last night hence sleep wasn't great and also resting today. Nothing crazy but enough to take it easy today.

notes: Real regression derived from thumbs-down feedback #13 / llm_call #315. The failed response acknowledged the rest day with good advice but did not call update_context to append to log.md. This extends coverage from chat_log_life_disruption (childcare disruption) to a social/lifestyle disruption causing the same missing log behavior.
